ops.title: FP Setup add-path hardening: two-budget readiness + fresh-domain teardown (v0.34.0)
ops.body:
Hardens the File Provider "add" (Setup) path after `ccp add` failed with ErrDomainNotServing: the FP appex cold-started ~4 min after Setup began, so a single 30s readiness deadline expired against an appex that wasn't answering yet, and the failed add left an orphaned registered domain retrying enumerations. Shipped in fusekit v0.34.0.

Fix (i) — two-budget readiness wait (overlay/provider.go waitDomainServes). Replaces the single 30s deadline with: a CONTACT budget (appReadyTimeout, default 2m) bounding the wait while the app answers ErrAppUnavailable (not up yet), then a SERVE budget (readyTimeout, default 6m) measured from the app's FIRST answered domain verdict, so a slow app spawn never eats the appex's materialization time. Either budget expiring still returns ErrDomainNotServing — the migrate-storm gate (never cut an account dir over to a domain that can't serve reads) is preserved, only made patient enough to outlast the observed cold start. ErrOpUnsupported/ErrCannotControl still fail immediately; no raw-filesystem fallback (a silent read would resurrect the TCC prompt storm). The outer context.WithTimeout was removed; termination is the per-budget deadline checks + ProbeDomain's own per-call deadline (AppClient.do sets it).

Fix (ii) — no orphan on Setup failure (fileproviderd/remote.go EnsureReport + overlay/provider.go Setup/cutOver). EnsureReport spawns the app, then determines fresh-vs-preexisting via a Path pre-check BEFORE Register. Because EnsureRunning only proves the control socket is dialable (not that the appex has loaded its OS-persisted domain list — getDomains is async), a raw single Path==ErrNoDomain is unsafe: a just-respawned appex could report a pre-existing domain as absent and Setup would then tear down a live domain. So the pre-check SETTLE-confirms: ErrNoDomain is only trusted as "fresh" if it persists across a short settle window (domainLoadSettle); if the domain appears (any answered non-ErrNoDomain verdict) fresh=false, stop early; any hard error → fresh=false (conservative — never tear down on uncertainty). Setup Removes the domain ONLY when THIS call freshly registered it and a later cutOver step failed; a pre-existing domain is left alone. cutOver seeds the private store dir BEFORE laying the account-dir symlink, so a failure never leaves a dangling symlink. Concurrency: Setup has no per-domain lock and assumes the consumer serializes Setup per account (cc-pool's pool.Manager per-account lock does).

Uses only the frozen proto-1 control ops (Path/Register/Remove); the sole new API is the additive overlay Spec field FileProviderSpec.AppReadyTimeout.
